source.title: A Retrospective, Multicenter Analysis Within the National Network Genomic Medicine Lung Cancer in Germany to Detect RET Fusions as a Possible Mechanism of Resistance in Patients With EGFR Mutations.

finding.description: RET fusions were detected at diagnosis in 2 patients and acquired in 7 patients during EGFR-TKI treatment, with a median time to detection of 11.4 months.

source_quote: RET fusions were detected at diagnosis in 2 patients and acquired in 7 patients during EGFR-TKI treatment, with a median time to detection of 11.4 months.

source.title: A Retrospective, Multicenter Analysis Within the National Network Genomic Medicine Lung Cancer in Germany to Detect RET Fusions as a Possible Mechanism of Resistance in Patients With EGFR Mutations.

finding.description: First-line treatment consisted of RET inhibition with pralsetinib in the 2 patients with atypical EGFR mutations, third-generation EGFR-TKIs in 6 patients (median PFS: 13.5 ± 9.2 months), and a second-generation EGFR-TKI (afatinib) in one patient (PFS: 8.7 months).

source_quote: First-line treatment consisted of RET inhibition with pralsetinib in the 2 patients with atypical EGFR mutations, third-generation EGFR-TKIs in 6 patients (median PFS: 13.5 ± 9.2 months), and a second-generation EGFR-TKI (afatinib) in one patient (PFS: 8.7 months).

source.title: A Retrospective, Multicenter Analysis Within the National Network Genomic Medicine Lung Cancer in Germany to Detect RET Fusions as a Possible Mechanism of Resistance in Patients With EGFR Mutations.

finding.description: Following progression, all patients underwent re-biopsy, confirming persistence of the EGFR mutation and presence of RET fusions. Second-line therapies varied, including chemo-immunotherapy (n = 4, mPFS: 6.5 ± 2.5 months), chemotherapy (n = 1, PFS: 0.3 months), and best supportive care (n = 1).

source_quote: Following progression, all patients underwent re-biopsy, confirming persistence of the EGFR mutation and presence of RET fusions. Second-line therapies varied, including chemo-immunotherapy (n = 4, mPFS: 6.5 ± 2.5 months), chemotherapy (n = 1, PFS: 0.3 months), and best supportive care (n = 1).

source.title: A Retrospective, Multicenter Analysis Within the National Network Genomic Medicine Lung Cancer in Germany to Detect RET Fusions as a Possible Mechanism of Resistance in Patients With EGFR Mutations.

finding.description: Three patients received the combined EGFR-TKI osimertinib and RET-TKI pralsetinib either in second- or third-line, with PFS ranging from 3.9 to 10.5 months.

source_quote: Three patients received the combined EGFR-TKI osimertinib and RET-TKI pralsetinib either in second- or third-line, with PFS ranging from 3.9 to 10.5 months.

source.title: A Retrospective, Multicenter Analysis Within the National Network Genomic Medicine Lung Cancer in Germany to Detect RET Fusions as a Possible Mechanism of Resistance in Patients With EGFR Mutations.

finding.description: Median OS for the cohort was 27 months (range: 7 to >30 months), with 4 patients still alive at last follow-up.

source_quote: Median OS for the cohort was 27 months (range: 7 to >30 months), with 4 patients still alive at last follow-up.
